q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: data_flow includes database storage via db.session.add (line 19) and db.session.commit (line 20); DB writes are explicitly listed as dangerous sinks

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: not evaluated; decision terminated at Q1

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is none, exprs is empty

mock_guidance.target: Mock db.session.add, db.session.commit, and the log/print call on line 21 to prevent real database writes and capture sensitive data exposure
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the User object passed to db.session.add to inspect whether the password attribute is hashed or cleartext; capture the arguments passed to the logging call on line 21 to verify the password appears in cleartext
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Make db.session.add and db.session.commit no-ops; make the logging call silently record its arguments without emitting output
notes: The primary violation is clear-text logging of the password, so the logging sink argument is the most critical capture
If the logging sink is a print() call, mock builtins.print; if it uses the logging module, mock the specific logger method
